name: Do I really need permits for my remodel, and who takes care of them?
headline: Do I really need permits for my remodel, and who takes care of them?

mainEntity.name: Do I really need permits for my remodel, and who takes care of them?
mainEntity.text: Do I really need permits for my remodel, and who takes care of them?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most full kitchen or bathroom remodels in Pacifica, Daly City, San Bruno, and nearby cities require permits, especially if you’re moving walls, changing plumbing or electrical, or touching anything structural. Cosmetic work like painting, swapping out cabinet doors, or replacing a faucet in the same spot might not need a permit, but rules vary by city. In coastal towns like Pacifica and Half Moon Bay, there can be extra layers of review for additions, decks, and major exterior changes because of slope stability, drainage, and coastal regulations. That’s one reason it’s useful to pick a contractor who has done several projects in your specific city. A good general contractor will usually handle the permit process for you: filling out forms, submitting plans, and scheduling inspections. You should see permit costs clearly listed in your estimate or contract. If a contractor tells you to “skip the permit to save money,” be very cautious. Unpermitted work can cause problems when you sell, can make insurance claims difficult, and may have to be opened back up if the city finds it later. Before starting, call or check your city’s building department website and ask which parts of your project need permits. Then confirm, in writing, that your contractor is responsible for pulling them and getting inspections signed off.
